post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 194: Old Timers Mafia!

Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123/the entire rest of the forum for the template

Story
One day, in the retiree home built by the TownLand-ian government for veterans of the Mafia Games (akin to the Hunger Games, but Mafia), trouble broke out. The old-timers were bored of watching the youngins fighting it out in the Mafia Games and wanted another go themselves. They asked the managers of the retiree home, benjaminsz (a current Mafia Games veteran competitor taking time off to work with the old timers) and NDMath (a rising star in the Mafia Games interning with benjaminsz), to authorize an Old Timers Game. They said no, you people are too old to be fighting. Whereupon the old-timers beat them to death with their canes to prove the point.
benjaminsz has been killed. He was the Chief Youngin.
NDMath has been killed. He was the Youngin Intern.


Rules
  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mod in charge.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This rule is fully open to reasonable interpretation by the mods.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, make a new post and use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not use the colors red (#f00), blue (#00f), green (#008000), purple (#8000BF), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
  • Voting/hangs
    • Votes must have the format Vote: <username>. Everything in your vote must be properly spaced, spelled, and capitalized (for further clarification on this, look at the “fdas requirements” for votes in other games). Please don’t argue about this in the thread, as I will not respond to any complaints about it.
    • You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No hang".
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • Any incorrect votecounts made will be counted as the correct votecount if no one points out the mistake within 3 days.
    • Do not fakevote. Fakevotes are punishable by warning.
    • If at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • If at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players (including the hanged player) may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins.

  • Activity
    • The moderator will state the length of a day at the beginning of every day. A day will normally be 3 real-life weeks. The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion, but this will be unlikely.
    • If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
    • To be ready to play, post the name of a player even older than the ones in this game.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Activity checks will be done over arbitrary time periods and either mod may do them whenever they want. This is to keep you on your toes. One check will be done at least every three real world days and no more often than once every 12 hours.
    • If a player does not post in the period between one activity check, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
    • If a player does not post in the period before the next activity check after receiving an in-game prod, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not post in the period before the next activity check receiving a PM prod, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. I will not prod you during a V/LA, but if you have a V/LA longer than about 10 days, please just ask for a replacement.
  • Since this is the old timers game, replacements suck. Please don’t get replaced.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language, or a censored version of it.
    • Do not make any personal attacks. Any clear personal attack will result in modkill.
    • Follow all of the rules.
    • Be mature.
    • Do not gamethrow. In other words, play towards your win condition.
    • Do not abuse loopholes. You know what I mean by the rules.
    • For a minor rule infraction, newbies (haha as if those exist in this game) can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction that would incur their last warning, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or forced-replaced (according to severity). Please do not make me modkill or force-replace you.
    • Do not argue with me about any warnings. Doing so will result in a modkill or a force-replacement.
  • As this is the old-timers game, I fully expect this section of the rules to be largely irrelevant. I really hope I am not disappointed.

  • Moderator Stuff
    • I (benjaminsz) am the moderator of Mafia Game 194.
    • NDMath is my co-moderator. He has all of the same powers as I do unless I overrule him in a specific situation, which should never happen.
    • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderator, as well as any generic flavor-related posts.
    • Green-colored text (#008000) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • Purple-colored text (#8000BF) is used for third party flavor-related posts.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
This game will use the C9++ setup. This is a semi-open setup.
  • There are 13 players.
  • This setup uses a Daystart.
  • Serial Killer chooses Investigation Immunity or 1-Shot Bulletproof at the start of the game.
  • Cops are guaranteed to be sane.
  • Cops get results in the form of Innocent/Guilty - SK's show up as Guilty unless they have Investigation Immunity.
  • Innocent Child is revealed at the start of Day 1 by the mod.
  • Doctors only prevent one night kill.
  • Kills are indistinguishable. There is no difference between a Mafia, Serial Killer, or Vigilante kill.
  • Mafia Roleblocker cannot kill and block in the same night.

Please look at the link for more information about the setup.

Role PMs
Vanilla Townie wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 108, a Vanilla Townie.

You played in a game where all town PR’s were killed early, but town proceeded to win using nothing but voting power. So you, too, will have to get by using nothing but voting power.

You have no night actions; your power comes from your freedom of speech and your vote.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town Cop wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 106, a Town Cop.

You played in a game where you were a cop, and your cop target of choice was a mafia. You were hailed as town’s savior in that game for this action, and you’re here to do it again.

Each night, you may PM me the name of one player to inspect. You will receive a result of innocent or guilty (town or not-town), or that you failed to inspect (you were somehow blocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town 1-Shot Cop wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 136, a Town 1-Shot Cop.

You were a one-shot cop in Game 136, when your uncanny sense of who to target helped town break the game open. You’re hoping you can get as lucky again, and once more use your one shot wisely. Do not miss your chance - this opportunity comes… twice in a lifetime?

One time, at night, you may PM me the name of one player to inspect. You will receive a result of innocent or guilty (town or not-town), or that you failed to inspect (you were somehow blocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town Doctor wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 123, a Town Doctor.

You played in a game in which you, personally, brought the hammer down on the last mafia. This experience has scarred you, and although you have since been hailed as a hero, you no longer have a thirst for violence. Thus, you have turned your attention to healing and protecting the weak, injured, and vulnerable.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player (including yourself) to protect. That night, any kills that target that player will fail (unless you are roleblocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town 1-Shot Doctor wrote:
You are a vetern of Game 105, a Town 1-Shot Doctor.

You were a doctor in your game, but were paralyzed with indecision for most of the game and didn’t manage to save anyone… that is, until your finest hour, when you managed to save someone from death at the twilight of the game and salvage it for the town. You have been known ever since as “The one-shot wonder”. However, several conspiracy theories have since emerged stating that your early-game inaction indicates that you were actually not a doctor at all, but a 1-shot doctor, although nothing has been proven. The game moderator reversed themself on this several times, so the answer may never be found.

One time, at night, you may PM me the name of a player (including yourself) to protect. That night, any kills that target that player will fail (unless you are roleblocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town Vigilante wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 102, a Town Vigilante.

You are legendary as the best vig in history, playing aggressively and decisively every night and eliminating the mafia in short order. Now, you’re back for more, certain of your capabilities and ready to dish out the death.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town 1-Shot Vigilante wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 168, a Town 1-Shot Vigilante.

You were a one-shot vig in Game 168, when your uncanny sense of who to target helped town break the game open. You’re hoping you can get as lucky again, and once more use your one shot wisely. Do not miss your chance - this opportunity comes… twice in a lifetime?

One time, at night, you may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town Innocent Child wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 130, a Town Innocent Child.

When they were setting up this game, there was initially a worry that, since everyone already knew each other’s roles from watching each other’s games, town would have an easy win. Then someone suggested a solution: Everyone would stop taking their memory pills the week before the game, so as to forget everything except their own games. Of course, no one can remember who suggested this, since it’s worked like a charm. What they all don’t know is, you suggested this. So you still have your memories of them… or rather, because of a mission you undertook with the medical staff, they still have their memories of you. For some reason no one can explain, everyone remembers your game, in which you were a well-respected and valuable VT who helped town win the game.

At the beginning of the game, you will be announced as the Innocent Child, which will confirm you as town.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town Mason wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 145/179, a Town Mason. Your partner(s) is/are <name> (and <name>).

Your game was one of the craziest games in history, as it was based on the video game Starcraft. As such, you and your old buddies from that game remember the game and its events very well - including each others’ innocent alignments. Though you all may have lost your weapons, abilities, and all your other perks from that game, you’re still determined to work together to help town win.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. You are confirmed town to your partners, and vice versa.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town Roleblocker wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 171, a Town Roleblocker.

You were a roleblocker in your game - a town roleblocker. Your history with roleblocking accuracy is a bit sketchy, as you were only partially helpful in your game. However, you are younger than anyone else here, so that should help your roleblocking efficacy.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player to roleblock. Unless someone interferes, that person's night action will become ineffective that night.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Town 1-Shot Roleblocker wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 114, a Town 1-Shot Roleblocker.

You were a roleblocker in your game. However, you were killed night one, so you only got to block one person. You are now so afraid of being killed again that you are certain that you will only have one chance to block someone. Better make it count. You only get one shot, do not miss your chance - this opportunity comes… twice in a lifetime?

One time, at night, you may PM me the name of a player to roleblock. Unless someone interferes, that person's night action will become ineffective that night.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 112, a Mafia Goon. Your partner(s) is/are <name>, a <role> (, and <name>, a <role>).

You played in a game in which the mafia won, and you’re proud to have been part of a victory for the bad guys. Now, you’re hungry to do it again. What can you say? You just love the deception and the murder. Go get ‘em, you elder baddie, you.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 120, a Mafia Roleblocker. Your partner(s) is/are <name>, a <role> (, and <name>, a <role>).

You were a mafia roleblocker in your game. You blocked a cop night action, helping mafia to a victory. It seemed so easy: All you have to do is just keep them distracted for a few hours… Now you’re back for more. Man, this role really makes mafia a snap, huh? Let’s hope it really is as easy as you remember...

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced. Each night you may also PM me the name of a player to roleblock. Unless someone interferes, that person's night action will become ineffective that night. You cannot perform a kill and roleblock in the same night.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Mafia Godfather wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 119, a Mafia Godfather. Your partner(s) is/are <name>, a <role> (, and <name>, a <role>).

You are well known as the greatest mafia player ever. No town had a chance of discovering you in your game; despite your mafia partners dying quickly, you won the game with seeming ease. Now, you’re here to do it again.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced. You are also immune to investigative roles.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.
Serial Killer wrote:
You are a veteran of Game 72, a Serial Killer.

You are well remembered as the strongest third party player in the history of the Mafia Games. Your SK victory is legendary, and you know that even in your old age, you are still the apex predator… at least out of these other old-timers. Time to extend your kill streak and maintain your position at the top of the all time leaderboard.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced. As soon as you see this PM, you must choose to be either Investigative Immune or 1-Shot Bulletproof.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and you are still alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.


Alive (3)
  • jeremylu
  • aroodman
  • pandabear10 mathjay Thornclaw9000

Dead (10)
  • Charge (Vanilla Townie)
  • atomicpianowitch (One-Shot Cop)
  • phi_ftw1619 (Vanilla Townie)
  • chess24 (Vanilla Townie)
  • fdas (One-Shot Vigilante)
  • jeffisepic (Vanilla Townie)
  • fishy15 (One-Shot Bulletproof Serial Killer)
  • knittingfrenzy18 (Mafia Godfather)
  • HannahW (Vanilla Townie)
  • Made_in_2004 (Vanilla Townie)


Significant Events
[*]Day One starts.
[*]Day One ends, Night One starts.
[*]Night One ends, Day Two starts.
[*]Day Two ends, Night Two starts.
[*]Night Two ends, Day Three starts.
[*]Day Three ends, Night Three starts.
[*]Night Three ends, Day Four starts.
[*]Day Four ends, Night Four starts.
[*]Night Four ends, Day Five starts.


Warnings
[*]Made_in_2004 has not received a warning for, and I quote, "no reason".


Do not post until this line is crossed out. UNLEASH THE HOUNDS!

One time, at night, you may PM me the name of a player (including yourself) to protect. That night, any kills that target that player will fail (unless you are roleblocked).

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Town Vigilante]You are a veteran of [color=#0f0]Game 102, a Town Vigilante[/color].

You are legendary as the best vig in history, playing aggressively and decisively every night and eliminating the mafia in short order. Now, you’re back for more, certain of your capabilities and ready to dish out the death.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Town 1-Shot Vigilante]You are a veteran of [color=#0f0]Game 168, a Town 1-Shot Vigilante[/color].

You were a one-shot vig in Game 168, when your uncanny sense of who to target helped town break the game open. You’re hoping you can get as lucky again, and once more use your one shot wisely. Do not miss your chance - this opportunity comes… twice in a lifetime? 

One time, at night, you may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Town Innocent Child]You are a veteran of [color=#0f0]Game 130, a Town Innocent Child[/color].

When they were setting up this game, there was initially a worry that, since everyone already knew each other’s roles from watching each other’s games, town would have an easy win. Then someone suggested a solution: Everyone would stop taking their memory pills the week before the game, so as to forget everything except their own games. Of course, no one can remember who suggested this, since it’s worked like a charm. What they all don’t know is, you suggested this. So you still have your memories of them… or rather, because of a mission you undertook with the medical staff, they still have their memories of you. For some reason no one can explain, everyone remembers your game, in which you were a well-respected and valuable VT who helped town win the game.

At the beginning of the game, you will be announced as the Innocent Child, which will confirm you as town.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Town Mason]You are a veteran of [color=#0f0]Game 145/179, a Town Mason[/color]. Your partner(s) is/are <name> (and <name>).

Your game was one of the craziest games in history, as it was based on the video game Starcraft. As such, you and your old buddies from that game remember the game and its events very well - including each others’ innocent alignments. Though you all may have lost your weapons, abilities, and all your other perks from that game, you’re still determined to work together to help town win.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. You are confirmed town to your partners, and vice versa.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Town Roleblocker]You are a veteran of [color=#0f0]Game 171, a Town Roleblocker[/color].

You were a roleblocker in your game - a town roleblocker. Your history with roleblocking accuracy is a bit sketchy, as you were only partially helpful in your game. However, you are younger than anyone else here, so that should help your roleblocking efficacy.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player to roleblock. Unless someone interferes, that person's night action will become ineffective that night. 

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Town 1-Shot Roleblocker]You are a veteran of [color=#0f0]Game 114, a Town 1-Shot Roleblocker[/color].

You were a roleblocker in your game. However, you were killed night one, so you only got to block one person. You are now so afraid of being killed again that you are certain that you will only have one chance to block someone. Better make it count. You only get one shot, do not miss your chance - this opportunity comes… twice in a lifetime?

One time, at night, you may PM me the name of a player to roleblock. Unless someone interferes, that person's night action will become ineffective that night. 

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one town member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Mafia Goon]You are a veteran of [color=#f00]Game 112, a Mafia Goon[/color]. Your partner(s) is/are <name>, a <role> (, and <name>, a <role>).

You played in a game in which the mafia won, and you’re proud to have been part of a victory for the bad guys. Now, you’re hungry to do it again. What can you say? You just love the deception and the murder. Go get ‘em, you elder baddie, you.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Mafia Roleblocker]You are a veteran of [color=#f00]Game 120, a Mafia Roleblocker[/color]. Your partner(s) is/are <name>, a <role> (, and <name>, a <role>).

You were a mafia roleblocker in your game. You blocked a cop night action, helping mafia to a victory. It seemed so easy: All you have to do is just keep them distracted for a few hours… Now you’re back for more. Man, this role really makes mafia a snap, huh? Let’s hope it really is as easy as you remember...

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced. Each night you may also PM me the name of a player to roleblock. Unless someone interferes, that person's night action will become ineffective that night. You cannot perform a kill and roleblock in the same night.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Mafia Godfather]You are a veteran of [color=#f00]Game 119, a Mafia Godfather[/color]. Your partner(s) is/are <name>, a <role> (, and <name>, a <role>).

You are well known as the greatest mafia player ever. No town had a chance of discovering you in your game; despite your mafia partners dying quickly, you won the game with seeming ease. Now, you’re here to do it again.

During the night, you may talk with your partner(s) in this quicktopic. Each night you or one of your partners may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced. You are also immune to investigative roles.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and there is at least one mafia member alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

[quote=Serial Killer]You are a veteran of [color=#9a00ff]Game 72, a Serial Killer[/color].

You are well remembered as the strongest third party player in the history of the Mafia Games. Your SK victory is legendary, and you know that even in your old age, you are still the apex predator… at least out of these other old-timers. Time to extend your kill streak and maintain your position at the top of the all time leaderboard.

Each night you may PM me the name of a player to kill. Unless someone interferes, on the following day the kill will be announced. As soon as you see this PM, you must choose to be either Investigative Immune or 1-Shot Bulletproof.

You win when all opposing factions are dead and you are still alive, or nothing can prevent that from happening.[/quote]

I only see NAI things here.

I get wanting to highlight faulty reasoning. I get that townreads can be just as important as scumreads. And if there were any Newbies in this game, I'd even acknowledge an educational value. But as there aren't any, defending a person who has been attacked, before said person has had a chance to speak for themselves? That's at best poor play, and at worst outright scummy. You're giving them an out, an excuse that they can fall back on to slip under the radar.

If all there was to an attack was the logic behind it, Mafia would be awfully boring. Fortunately, that's only half the battle: the other half is the reaction, the rebuke, the rebuttal from the accused. By stepping in and cutting this process short, you're making scumhunting unnecessarily difficult.

Anyway, this is part of a larger and rather disconcerting trend I've noticed in this game so far of people just...dismissing arguments and moving on. People slamming this as NAI and that as NAI all over the place, and then just...stopping there, without much more to add. What do you expect to achieve from that? Again, I understand being cautious about tunneling, but it's far too early in the game to worry about losing focus.

I believe this attitude is generally detrimental to us Town; on the other hand, too many people are acting this way in order for all of them to be scum, which is why I'm bringing it up in this more general fashion. I think we will be more effective at hunting scum if we don't give them such easy outs as these, so this is as much a call to action for those of you who are behaving like this but are actually Town as much as it is a scummy red flag.

But I recognize that this may be a generational gap—perhaps dismissing observations as NAI is particularly trendy—and if so, do speak up. I'd love to hear some other thoughts on this. Regardless, I'm keeping my eye out for this kind of behavior.

I would like to respond to neil in post 124

Yes, I am a strong believer in pressuring people to be able to read them better. In fact that's what my scumhunting is mainly based around in my previous games. However, and yes I do speak from *relatively recent* experience, that it is also necesarry for people to comment on reads as well. And I agree with you that if you find something scummy and others just tell you "NAI" it might be annoying, but they're speaking from their point of views as well, and in mafia everyone should speak their POV as well. Their are reads that I agree with, and reads I don't agree with, and I hope that you only listen to the opinions that fit what you're saying and disregard the opposing ones- after all, these interactions are useful to analyze late game.

I do agree with you, at a certain point calling EVERYTHING simply NAI can get very suspicious real quickly, however relatively early in the game where many things are just getting started I do think marking posts as not alignment indicative is, well, not alignment indicative. Besides, not every post is either scummy or towny, and it's pretty hard to develop a conclusion based on this little posts so far.

This is my take 2 days into the game, but if this trends continues, then I will gradually put my head into where you're thinking, as that's just calling fencesitting- where you let the game continue by staying on the fence.

post_rendered:
NeilOnnsu wrote:
I get wanting to highlight faulty reasoning. I get that townreads can be just as important as scumreads. And if there were any Newbies in this game, I'd even acknowledge an educational value. But as there aren't any, defending a person who has been attacked, before said person has had a chance to speak for themselves? That's at best poor play, and at worst outright scummy. You're giving them an out, an excuse that they can fall back on to slip under the radar.

If all there was to an attack was the logic behind it, Mafia would be awfully boring. Fortunately, that's only half the battle: the other half is the reaction, the rebuke, the rebuttal from the accused. By stepping in and cutting this process short, you're making scumhunting unnecessarily difficult.

Anyway, this is part of a larger and rather disconcerting trend I've noticed in this game so far of people just...dismissing arguments and moving on. People slamming this as NAI and that as NAI all over the place, and then just...stopping there, without much more to add. What do you expect to achieve from that? Again, I understand being cautious about tunneling, but it's far too early in the game to worry about losing focus.

I believe this attitude is generally detrimental to us Town; on the other hand, too many people are acting this way in order for all of them to be scum, which is why I'm bringing it up in this more general fashion. I think we will be more effective at hunting scum if we don't give them such easy outs as these, so this is as much a call to action for those of you who are behaving like this but are actually Town as much as it is a scummy red flag.

But I recognize that this may be a generational gap—perhaps dismissing observations as NAI is particularly trendy—and if so, do speak up. I'd love to hear some other thoughts on this. Regardless, I'm keeping my eye out for this kind of behavior.

Unfortunately I think I might be primarily responsible for this. This has been something I did a lot and it has been pointed out to me that I should stop this. But some things trigger me too much like accusations of wishy washiness. But the main problem for me is that I have ended up becoming very cynical about things and lots of things no longer seem scummy to me because I have seen town do those very things.

post_rendered:
Thoughts on openings prior to #52
Neil is helpful, confident
Charge seems defensive, playful, engaged
Pandabear10 is being a typical himself
Knittingfrenzy is clearly having fun but also taking a back seat. Does not seem too scummy though
Summitwei is quintuple posting, can’t get a good read


The game starts at #52, when Neil ignites discussion talking about fluff. This gives off a towny vibe, not only because he is starting discussion but he is clear, focused, and attempts to collaborate. Charge defends himself two posts later, and his tone seems innocent, but his defenses are repeated and excuse-making, which is scummy. Most of the following posts are about fluff/Charge etc. and none of it really seems to get anywhere.
E comes in at #61 with a typical e post. This is just his opening, not much contribution here. The comments are light, almost like short things you would say when chatting in a big circle. However, jeff steers things back on track a little in #63 asking about Charge’s town meta. Jeff gets a townread on Charge because he sees that Charge’s fluff is town-indicative.
It’s #79, and Charge is still posting about the fluff scumreads. The defensiveness here is getting a little suspicious not because it’s intense but because Charge continues to bring it up or at least respond to it.
Knittingfrenzy makes a real post in #83, and while it’s not entirely contributive, the tone feels extremely comfortable (and the content is mostly light talk anyway) so town points just based off of tone for this one.
In #93, Neil goes after e a little bit for repeating what everyone else has already said. This is understandable. E’s post felt like fake contribution. Neil looks good for calling it out, although Charge mentioned something about it not long before, so that does not hold up for Neil. It looks hypocritical.
Aroodman comes in at #97, not much to see here, has a reasonably towny outlook on the game in their opening but nothing too telling in either direction. Town-leaning
Panda has a very long post in #105, and I would call about ⅓ of it useful. I know that pandbear likes to have a lot of fun but it feels like pandbear is not taking very many opinions, nor is he encouraging teamwork. I will give him points for encouraging talking with the people he is attacking rather than simply calling them out, but the scummy things about this post outweigh the towny. Also, he called himself a hypocrite for calling someone out when they weren’t contributing. This implies that panda was fully aware that he was not contributing. This is scummy not because of the lack of contribution but because of the fact that panda was so well aware of his flaws and what he needed to work on. This type of thing appears in town mainly for newbies, and is scummier as people gain more experience as they learn to be comfortable and helpful as town, something I did not learn for a long time. Gonna give a Vote: pandabear10
Aroodman has his first contribution-like thing in #113, despite the towny tone he does not take any points, start any discussion regarding alignment, etc. He has yet to get very involved in this game so there’s not much to read, but like pandabear he is not looking great. I would look to see more, I also don’t know who you are lol.
RTG in #114 and #116 has some nice comments about the game. I like his point about red (I assume that’s knittingfrenzy??) being able to read Neil, if she ends up being town she could be a vital asset. Still haven’t seen much from her yet though. RTG also talks about Charge denying being towny, this seemed to be a new point. As for tone, RTG seems down-to-earth if that makes sense, like he’s not taking BS and he’s putting himself on a level where he can chat and work things out. The tone and the subject matter give me a townread on RTG so far
Summit in #119 votes for RTG, saying someone’s gotta be the first one on a wagon, I don’t understand this?? I don’t remember any reasons summit stated previously for scumreading RTG at all.
Neil is progressively aggressive in #120 and #121, same old thing we’ve seen all game and only further cements his agreed-upon towniness.
E in #123 responds to things mostly, no new content yet again. He does make a nice defense of himself though in response to Neil saying he was bandwagoning. He talks about how working together is useful, this is kind of the one black sheep in Neil’s play thus far. It’s less important now since we’re in the beginnings of day one but Neil takes an opposing stance on most things, rather than an agreeing one, and while opinions are opinions working together is also important. This hardly makes e any townier but I think it’s an important point to think about in the future especially towards the end of the day.
Neil pens an absolutely beautiful post in #124. He gives several examples, one of them from me, of players excusing other players from attacks. He says what is up with this, it’s scummy behavior. He explains that it’s become more of the meta, for people to dismiss attacks as NAI instead of working together from evidence. This is something I have been guilty of for a long time whoopsie. This post takes a stance never taken in the game before, and yet it is so responsive and relevant.
Jeff responds to it, he kind of agrees but also points out how everyone should take their own points and opinions and that not all posts are indicative of alignment. He also asks Neil if he can powerwolf
Summit jeff and neil talk about some stuff, jeff seems to be working well with Neil, and at this point all three seem to be listening more than speaking (except summitwei) so there ya go. They’re just gathering things early day 1 and it’s pretty towny

Neil, I would like to work with you. I don’t know if the wishes are mutual but you have a high spot on my townreads. post_rendered:
@Neil
I personally dislike micro reads unless they're scumslips. Pings may be pings, but I think that these small pings do not amount to much in the overall read of each player - to some degree, every player has ping-y content. Thus I find trying to call a person a scum because of the "tone" or one-liners in one or two posts more of an excuse to vote said person than an actual read.

Also, I acknowledge that meta can be useful, but I must say that this game is full of old players who haven't been back in around a year. Your styles have probably changed more or less, so I think looking at past meta games and judging anomalies from these games as scummy isn't reasonable (ie Panda contributing apparently made him less towny on first impression). *Another thing about meta: I'm not sure how many people are going to read past games, but if only a few people do, it's really easy to get away with sheeping by saying "because their usual town meta is different" etc.

One of the ways I used to advocate to move forward is to put pressure on every person turn by turn and see how they react. I personally think that analyzing the "town leaders" is one of the best courses of actions to take, so that if we judge them as town we can trust their opinions early on. Also, like someone said, in D1, interactions aren't exactly useful. However, recently, I've discovered it is a good idea to list interactions that you may think are not w/w to compile a fairly accurate POE list by, let's say, D3.

Next, regarding the D1 hang, I think hanging a fairly scummy person with many interaction routes or a PL would be the best. I'm not sure, but I think recently I've acquired the idea that a D1 hang is more to set up scum-catching in later days rather than to guess a scum (well, of course, hang the obvs scum if there is one). I know it is a bit early to be talking about hangs, but I want to make my stance clear.

Also, you've posted posts on other players, and since I recall you talking about how bystanders shouldn't jump in and help, I was wondering if I should keep my opinions, if defensive, to myself. Otherwise, I may have some input on the other players, which may in your words give them an easy way out.

@fdas
Now this is quite the interesting read on panda. I see your point about scum completely omitting scummy statements in general, even though I'm not quite sure about panda's meta. What do you think about the whole argument about panda's fluff and non=contribution?

On summit's vote: this has repeatedly been asked by many people to no avail, so I see no point in you re-asking it other than to pretend you're contributing.

As for the reaction-test excuse, I'm pretty sure we're all aware of this and thus this is just stating the obvious.

How did I conduct myself well? About 80% of my actions at one point were dictated by passive-aggressiveness and I bet Kage would hate me if he were the target of this. Also, you have this great big paragraph about me... only to end up with no conclusion. Excusez-moi? This is the epitome of fake contribution.

Basically your #169 is about 80% not contribution, ie scummy, even though I like your point about panda.

apw
First of all, isn't me taking a strong note of how I'm viewed inherently scummy? Or is self-consciousness degree also a part of meta and thus less AI now? IDK I'm just confused how broad-spectrum-obvious-stuff like this is less AI then small subtle things like Neil's point about you caring more about the game than you let on.

Also, I slightly disagree with not analyzing interactions D1. It's not completely pointless, especially if you can become pretty sure that a group of people are not w/w. Perhaps it might not help now, but I think more solid interactions are helpful to save for oncoming days.

Neil, I'll grant you some of this. I feel that this is just a rift in philosophy. From my experience, third parties (meaning people other than the attacker and the accused) voicing their opinions before the response of the accused does not take away the accused burden to respond, and we do watch to see if the accused is wagoning off what someone else is saying. Heck, there's been discussion already about wagoning on attacks (if not necessarily defense, but I recall that happening). V/LA Sat-Mon this weekend. retreat without internet

My panda vote I originally thought was going to put three votes on them so perhaps they would feel pressure. instead they dismissed it as "you guys have no good reasoning" which is absolutely true, I voted them with reasoning I just debunked in the same post. I wasn't serious. Unvote

People's main complaint about me so far is "not contributing." I'm just not willing to post pointless "analysis". I'm only going to say what I feel like I can back up, or else post intentional thoughts. I think that in D1 so far the analysis of people's words are pretty useless, because nobody is going to scumtell if they can, and if you do, or don't, then you enter into WIFOM. Is being unsure scummy? Or towny? Is being stupid scummy? or towny? is being frustrated or aggressive tell you anything about someone? Not usually. And if so, I say wifom.

What I think can be analyzed is in the relationships people make with each other through conversation; not necessarily whether they have "buddying" or related roles or know anything about each other (in this setup i believe only masons or mafia do this and it would be silly to KNOW anything about this from an observer standpoint yet), but rather how you engage in discussion. What I'm saying is: talking about how someone's talking is a lot less useful than talking to them.

I'm personally having a HARD TIME not going to lie with reading comprehension because there is so much going on and I can't draw all the lines. I'm going to keep rereading to try and get it but there is a lot of stuff that I find meaningless in between that I am trying to filter out while I look for the useful stuff. Again, I don't expect to find anything readable yet.

@Neil: are their reactions pointless? Nooo, I think that their reactions are misguided. Could it say something about them? Sure. Have they been baited? yes. Like I said, I appreciate you're trying to draw people out which I realize I haven't done so far so I am starting to copy you and do my best here

@summit: What makes day 1 harder? uhh see above paragraph about wifom. maybe it's lack of recent experience, but I can't think of many important things day 1 that could not be reduced to wifom.

post_rendered:
Hey all I'm back, I found time for this thing.
From what I remember before finals is that panda was scummy, Neil was towny, but he didn't want to work with me. Red was towny to me but not to everyone else. Summit seemed kinda genuine. Charge was acting fluffy and defensive then went full analysis mode. This aligns with my notes from that part of the game so let's dive into things

Something I notice right off the bat about e's 175 is that he votes two people in the same post, seemingly inadvertently, as if he forgot his first vote. This strikes me as towny, as advanced scum players tend to have a certain agenda they are trying to push, and clearly with the wild, mindless voting e is giving us the fact that there is no agenda as of yet. At least an e agenda. This is just something I notice that I'm putting in the book.
One person I don't have strong read on yet is RTG. RTG so far I've found your posts to be kind of bottomless. Baseless I guess? There's not a lot to go off of but they just feel like the icing on some sort of cake. Reading further I will be looking into RTG as he is one I have not figured out yet

Also as a town team we're not doing great. Like let's be honest here.
From what I remember about my fun/strong games as town, I was having a lot of positive discussion. I remember discussing specific people, bouncing ideas and reaching conclusions (see 156, 160, 161). Now it kind of seems like we're missing the mark. Obviously things are kind of chaotic D1 but they don't have to be like this. The general vibe I am getting is kinda feeling lost. We're not reaching very many conclusions/consensuses, and people are arguing about rather pointless things. Something I've been thinking about lately is how mafia is a social game. Like sure we like to analyze posts and stuff but at the end of a day the winning and losing teams are decided by who can focus, who can work together. The general vibe of a game often dictates how players act. Like a bad party is like a flat soda, it just kinda flops. You feel like it can be better but there's nothing you can do. The vibe of a bad party just gets everyone down and makes the situation worse.
So what I'm trying to think about is how we can all work together to come together to some conclusions. I'm not nearly as experienced/mature as most of y'all so this is a great time to work together but yeah. Those are my shower thoughts. This is why I think Neil is towny because I feel like the things he says are clear and directed and he doesn't try to fog the situation nor does he try to spread fluff.
Also, for those of you taking time out of your life to read this, don't respond how I often see you respond. I'm not great with describing with words, but I feel like a lot of responses to thoughts like this are... inconclusive. Almost like they're waiting for another voice without asking for opinions or discussion. You give your opinion on things, it is most often critical, and then the masses applaud that contribution. What happens next? Another person will provide their opinions on the first opinions, most often critical, it is called contribution. We end up in some sort of vicious cycle. So please refrain from your usual path and instead act. I will discuss with you. Things can be talked about in unbiased, level ways that go somewhere new with each post that is made. Or at least respond to other's peoples opinions with things that acknowledge what they said and spark good things. Another solution to this problem is to make contribution that does not milk any pointless opinions. My comment above is my example of this.
So there's my stream of consciousness. You might be noticing that I'm not quite right in the head but I promise I'm doing my best at things like this.
Yes I am going to bed now but I will be back tomorrow. Ready to play the game instead of just talk about it. I just thought that needed to be said

panda 148 wrote:
Neil, I'll grant you some of this. I feel that this is just a rift in philosophy. From my experience, third parties (meaning people other than the attacker and the accused) voicing their opinions before the response of the accused does not take away the accused burden to respond, and we do watch to see if the accused is wagoning off what someone else is saying. Heck, there's been discussion already about wagoning on attacks (if not necessarily defense, but I recall that happening). As long as the material is not just fluff, it's useful as further analysis for interactions between more people.

i think it's also just people tending to be more active than previously, so they want something to do and will respond to literally anything even if it's not about them. people tend to take one side or the other leading to two groups essentially forming on every attack, and there are really 3 possible responses:

1. defend early, of course if it turns out you're defending scum then you get accused of buddying but if you're defending town it's null -- largely just wifom anyway
2. attack early, if it turns out you're attacking town it's kinda null but if you're attacking scum then you're either bussing (unlikely) or useful town (more likely) and therefore more probable to be town
3. lurk and wait for further responses so you can provide a more unbiased response -- this is what was mentioned as the previous meta before this generational gap

People's main complaint about me so far is "not contributing." I'm just not willing to post pointless "analysis". I'm only going to say what I feel like I can back up, or else post intentional thoughts. I think that in D1 so far the analysis of people's words are pretty useless, because nobody is going to scumtell if they can, and if you do, or don't, then you enter into WIFOM. Is being unsure scummy? Or towny? Is being stupid scummy? or towny? is being frustrated or aggressive tell you anything about someone? Not usually. And if so, I say wifom.

What I think can be analyzed is in the relationships people make with each other through conversation; not necessarily whether they have "buddying" or related roles or know anything about each other (in this setup i believe only masons or mafia do this and it would be silly to KNOW anything about this from an observer standpoint yet), but rather how you engage in discussion. What I'm saying is: talking about how someone's talking is a lot less useful than talking to them.

I'm personally having a HARD TIME not going to lie with reading comprehension because there is so much going on and I can't draw all the lines. The second paragraph here is I believe something along the lines of what I have said recently. However I feel like I've got to change my opinion on this because I had a realization, maybe Red will agree. 
Sure, wifom, pointless arguing, all the stuff we say has been clogging up the thread might seem pointless, but think about it like RVS. RVS is completely pointless. The stuff that goes down in the first 50 or so posts most often has nothing to do with the rest of the game. One could say that we should eliminate RVS completely, as it is fluffy and accomplishes nothing. However, if it was eliminated, there would be literally nothing else to go off of. Panda, you have pointed out a valid flaw in my argument, you have my respect for that, lemme explain.
Back when I was in middle school, when I entered 8th grade, a ninth grader who went to my same school texted me and asked me "Hey how was your first week of 8th grade??" I responded with "eh it was kinda boring." She then said "yeah, that's what it was for us, and that's what all the other classes have said too." Then when I went to high school I asked an 8th grader what he thought of his first week, and he as well said it was boring.
The reason for this is that for 7 years leading up to 8th grade, kids see 8th graders at the top of the school. They have adventures, they're cool, they date, etc. etc. etc. and it's like one big middle school party before going into high school. And so kids go into 8th grade expecting all this fun, and the fun comes, but obviously not in the first week. The fun comes later in the year, at surprising moments.
So yeah, obviously the awesome town discussion I am remembering from D3 of a couple specific games is not going to come on early D1 in a game full of rusty college students. Although I think we can tweak our approach, my standards were a little off.

Panda, you have pointed out a valid flaw in my argument, you have my respect for that, lemme explain. 
Back when I was in middle school, when I entered 8th grade, a ninth grader who went to my same school texted me and asked me "Hey how was your first week of 8th grade??" I responded with "eh it was kinda boring." She then said "yeah, that's what it was for us, and that's what all the other classes have said too." Then when I went to high school I asked an 8th grader what he thought of his first week, and he as well said it was boring. 
The reason for this is that for 7 years leading up to 8th grade, kids see 8th graders at the top of the school. They have adventures, they're cool, they date, etc. etc. etc. and it's like one big middle school party before going into high school. And so kids go into 8th grade expecting all this fun, and the fun comes, but obviously not in the first week. The fun comes later in the year, at surprising moments. 
So yeah, obviously the awesome town discussion I am remembering from D3 of a couple specific games is not going to come on early D1 in a game full of rusty college students. Although I think we can tweak our approach, my standards were a little off.[quote=phi_ftw1618]

To answer your second question as to why I asked you about him specifically, I did not put a lot of thought into it. As for how I actually feel about aroodman: I agree with many of the points you stated: he is concise, not fluffing up the thread with obligatory analysis, he is encouraging people to speak up and asking questions, etc. I would say I townread him in the same I mentioned townreading jeff up above. However he's been a little lacking lately, he came in at #301 with a simple answer to a question and did not acknowledge anything that was going on at the time, kind of a change from his active play from before. Yes life gets in the way but there was no acknowledgement, no "I'll look at this later" or something, so that kind of struck me as off. Throughout the game I feel like he hasn't exactly been *there*, do you know what I'm saying? He responds to some posts, and they look great, but some areas and topics of the game seem to go ignored. At this point I'm not sure what to make of it honestly, whether it's cherrypicking or just a reserved playstyle. So my read on aroodman right now would be a tentative scumread. I think he has the attitude in each individual post of a town, but the way he is putting that contributive attitude (or lack there of) towards this game seems to be the problem.

post_rendered:
Hi double posting. It's way too late to do an analysis right now. However I've got free time tomorrow.
I encourage everybody to try to make time for a read-through (or analysis) of a specific person or one part of the game. I've found that just reading, without even trying to analyze, can help me get more motivated and back into a game. Reading through one person specifically can also help understand what's going on with everyone alignment-wise. Not deepfrying anybody is better than getting a random fry going that has no reasoning/support.

I've been noticing people LOVE to ask questions. Not just to specific people, but like, there's so many questions that are so transparently reaction test-esque thing that at some point I'm tempted just to call like every question of that notice a scumread since it's such an easy way to try and generate content without trying that much.

Just say a question. Hey, PERSON, why did you do this thing? No I'm not gonna try and analyze it myself, I'm just going to ask questions and wait for a reaction and tunnel on that instead.

Sure, their reactions CAN generate knowledge, but like these questions seem to be just brushed off, like they weren't there to actually scumhunt; just give the appearance of such. Here's the one example that stood out to me the most.
summitwei wrote:
@fdas: ignoring the meta knowledge stuff, what's your current read on charge? you said stuff like that post seemed towny but i'm unsure where your head is at overall
(i say to ignore the meta-knowledge stuff so you don't get to hard hedge. i don't expect this to be your actual read due to that)
fdas clearly has read this post; he replies to a post after this one. And summitwei clearly has seen that fdas read the question but did not respond, as evidence by him posting a bit later. However, let's look at summitwei's post:
summitwei wrote:
pandabear10 wrote:
Oh I just realized that's worded horribly

I didn't mean the attacks themselves are NAI, I meant that I thought that a lot of people were tunneling Charge and that might be AI, but too many people are tunneling Charge for it to be AI. Not that the attacks aren't invalid; I wasn't commenting on that.
this still doesn't make sense to me but it's a relatively minor point and i can't remember why i mentioned it in the first place so :shrug:

phi's above post (esp. the point regarding panda) also looks pure to me; i can elaborate but i'm sleep deprived rn
No mention of fdas. No mention of the question. Just completely ignores it.

Note that summitwei in a later post asks some more questions rather than doing much himself:
summitwei wrote:
@apw hi; i'm glad you're trying to motivate people. give me a read on someone whose alignment hasn't really been talked about much?
(actually this is a good exercise for everyone but i especially want to hear it from apw. personally phi=town is my only strong pre-reread read and i don't think anybody else has even mentioned him being town/scum)
Let I mean, sure he tries to give an example, but it's of someone who is clearly very much townread, rather than actually answering his own question.
Also, as a bit of a sidenote, I don't recall summitwei ever doing this weird question thing at all before this game. It pinged me a bit, but actually analyzing it showed me why this pinged me.
For that, Vote: summitwei
pandabear10 wrote:
I'm more in the 'mindset' of I'm uncertain of Charge's play. He(?) is a mixed bag, some towny things and scummy things. But you gotta defend yourself, which Charge has done. I'm just saying that if you don't defend yourself, I'm scumreading you.
My basis for saying "because you sound like you just don't want to defend yourself, which is the easy way out for scum" is that town always has a good reason for doing what they do. Scum don't. Town just needs to be honest. Scum are far more inclined to "take the easy way out."
So you're saying even with how much Charge has posted, you don't have ANY sort of concrete read on her? Also, (sorta unrelated to the meat of my response, but) just because scum are more inclined to do something doesn't make that something a certain scumread.
Quote:
Fair enough. You're highlighting that I am indeed uncertain about what I was saying. Because I am uncertain about that statement - did it come across differently? I was looking for Neil to respond to that.
I wasn't highlighting that. I was highlighting the FMPOV part of your post.
Quote:
Neil questioned several people earlier for defending others instead of letting people defend themselves. He was tempted to call people scum for it, but acknowledged that too many people were doing so for everyone that was doing it to actually be scum, so NAI.

So I was riding off the same reasoning on people tunneling Charge. Normally I'd think I was AI, but too many people are doing it, so tunneling Charge is now an ineffective AI = NAI.
Wait but you can't just say that was Neil's ONLY reason for saying that is NAI. Because it wasn't; he gave other reasons. While you basically cite that more people doing this one thing is the sole reason for it being NAI.
atomicpianowitch wrote:
Hi Charge. You seem to be making a lot of defenses for yourself, in fact an amount that I would consider excessive. Perhaps this has been talked about with you before but I would like to ask you why? Like I get a little defense that's important but would you agree that there's a certain point it becomes meaningless?
Shouldn't you know the answer to this question? You basically answered it yourself. Defense is important. You said that. Is excessive defense even bad?
atomicpianowitch wrote:
It appears that I need to explain/defend myself on many cases. In general I try not to defend myself too much, I have discovered that often it is better to leave the attack be, say "okay I made a mistake" and then get on with finding scum. I have heard tales of communities where defending yourself is completely out of the ordinary. However it seems that I am being called on to defend myself and plus there's just some general explaining to do about what I've said
When I first read atomicpianowitch's posts I always found them weird, but not really scummy since I just didn't understand them that much? But this quote: "I have heard tales of communities where defending yourself is completely out of the ordinary." is one of the most scummy excuses I've heard to not do something. You know the AoPS community. And even if you didn't before this game, you've seen people defending. So this looks to just be an excuse on why you didn't do something. "leave the attack?" What type of strategy is that? It's important to defend as it gives people insight into your reasons for posting, which helps advance the game.

I probably have votes on a bit too many people right now, but it's still within reason, so Vote: atomicpianowitch.

As for Charge overall, I found her scummy early D1, and this post is a lean scummy. Charge was early D1 because of her over-defensiveness. Life got the better of me this past week but I’m back now. I’ve only got about an hour at the moment and missed like over 100 posts, though, so I’m going to read over all of them, address the most recent points, then go back and unearth some of the things that happened a while ago.

Okay first off, I’m really sick of this large-series-of-quotes-and-put-little-comments-after-everything style of posting that panda/phi/probably some others are posting. Back in my day it was thing fdas did as scum pretending to be Town. Now people are seriously embracing it as a valid contributory technique? Assuming you are in fact Town, what exactly do you intend to achieve that way? How do you expect other Town members who read it to gain something useful if your individual comments are just disorganized and hidden amongst random others? How do you expect people to take your attacks seriously if you’re just going to jump to another topic so soon afterwards? Like, it was so easy to just ignore everything panda questioned me about because his post was just so lacking in focus. You gotta streamline things if you wanna have any chance of convincing others and demanding responses.

Anyway, I acknowledge this is again a playstyle thing rather than a scum hunting thing, but I wanna win and that means making sure the Town folk left behind at LyLo know what they’re doing.

More to come, I’m still on like #243 oops

Panda, you have pointed out a valid flaw in my argument, you have my respect for that, lemme explain.
Back when I was in middle school, when I entered 8th grade, a ninth grader who went to my same school texted me and asked me "Hey how was your first week of 8th grade??" I responded with "eh it was kinda boring." She then said "yeah, that's what it was for us, and that's what all the other classes have said too." Then when I went to high school I asked an 8th grader what he thought of his first week, and he as well said it was boring.
The reason for this is that for 7 years leading up to 8th grade, kids see 8th graders at the top of the school. They have adventures, they're cool, they date, etc. etc. etc. and it's like one big middle school party before going into high school. And so kids go into 8th grade expecting all this fun, and the fun comes, but obviously not in the first week. The fun comes later in the year, at surprising moments.
So yeah, obviously the awesome town discussion I am remembering from D3 of a couple specific games is not going to come on early D1 in a game full of rusty college students. Although I think we can tweak our approach, my standards were a little off.

post_rendered:
i'm on mobile, so no formal quotes. if i say that something happens but don't give a location you can ask me. i'm not doing a pbpa because they're wastes of time; please don't call cherry picking or something dumb like that.
also i'm writing this case while doing the reading. only way i can keep track of it

a few of charge's early posts feel pretty awkward to me. don't want to put too much weight into it (since awkward for me can still be normal for someone else) but it's at least worth noting.

the entire claiming towniness debacle is something i originally thought wouldn't be very notable but now i'm pretty sure it is. it gets to the point where charge is basically attacking herself at several points to make her cases. this feels like a twtbtwtbaw moment (basically scum trying to do something town would do because it's too weird for scum to do it). but i really don't have a satisfying explanation for why; i'll continue thinking though

charge's analyses (137 is the first example) tend to have an interesting trend: almost all of the reads mentioned are scum rather than town. when they are townreads (see the reference to 84 in that message) they're incredibly short and unexplained. there's a pretty obvious scum!charge explanation here: attacking people is good, but don't want to purely attack people since you'll get called out for that, so do the bare minimum of townreading. i should go and check to see if this is how charge plays as town but not sure if i'll have time for it
after writing this i did remember that the aforementioned 137 was supposed to be almost satirical. perhaps some better examples would be better but the trend holds
i guess the question from there is how *bad* this really is? and i feel like it should be bad though i'm pretty sure i've seen town do this before. don't think it's a nullifying factor though; i still don't like it

adding to this: there are some townreads gradually (e.g. on panda in 184). but the progression on panda feels sudden, going from a very careful "isn't scummy" to "genuinely towny" quite quickly. writing a note to keep an eye out for other weird progressions

i'm also quite confused by charge's cases since many of the arguments i see are not very good? like there are many things that are, to me, clearly not "fake contribution" that get blanket slapped under that tag. the example i saw just now was the response to fdas in #178. (on a side note, i remember at least a few other people also doing this stuff? maybe panda? and i wanted to ask a broader question about it at some point but i don't think i ever did so. anyways end side note)
this kind of occurrence comes out of an "attacking-for-the-sake-of-attacking" mentality quite often; i shouldn't have to explain why that's bad.

i'm getting to the point where "fancy omgus" is getting mentioned; i don't think that's bad by itself. what is bad is when you do that, get like one townread, and barely even try to look at anybody else

i see a read list in #321! and it's disappointing. i get one more town read that's completely unexplained (which isn't something i'd expect from charge) and a "i'm not scumreading hang candidates" which feels like the definition of keeping options open. (although by that point i can't even tell who "hang candidates" refers to? that could change things depending on if i'm misreading)

aaaaand that seems to be it; the main case against charge is that her reads are very much negative and don't seem to be coming from the motivation to hunt scum. it's definitely not a good look and i can say i'm definitely ok with this hang happening

(i'll probably end up voting apw at the end of the day; due to deadline hang being deadline hang, i'm not optimistic that a charge hang can happen. lots of stuff worth keeping in mind for tomorrow though)


future summit note that charge-fdas is too easy to be right but still might be right; some *weird* interactions between the two. won't read into this yet but should put it somewhere and i'm typing here rn sooooo

(sidenote: i saw #261 and that answers the question i had about the metaprogression on aroodman. thanks summit for the great explanation to your own question)



uh now that i've written a bunch of nonsense time to post it without proofreading cuz it's past midnight and i want sleep so i don't silly every other question on amc tmrw

I’ll explain what I can:

1. I felt like activity was low and that it would be good to have a D1 hang rather than no-hanging. Of course Charge self-hammered so this wasn’t a problem.
2. Strategies to try and get any sort of utility from apw had come up and while the information would be valuable, apw instead served to bait night kills.
3. I also realized that apw did indeed still have utility if he baited the kill and hanging correctly or mishanging a non-PR would be no worse in terms of overnight utility.

While the claim couldn’t be verified until the death, I’m surprised how quickly fdas jumped to conclusions about the hang. It’s also worth noting that jony jumped off the above argument against me really quickly, which is not something I’ve seen him do in the past.

I’m also surprised how quickly setup speculation has begun. I feel like the “no roleblocker” setup with many Ts could be an overnight scripted play by scum or SK to explain why only one kill occurred. I’m a bit suspicious about how quickly they jumped to conclusions when there could be SK hitting apw as well as scum or other blocking roles at play preventing multiple kills.

post_rendered:
Okay, so basically I've been sorta just ranting to Charge about this game on Discord (keep in mind I told her not to respond to anything, it's just ranting sorta helps me) since I was just sick of being attacked and I figured since she had some experience being attacked, she would understand me a bit better. Me talking to her has honestly given me motivation to continue playing instead of just gamethrowing or just replacing out.

The thing is, I don't get how this is necessarily a force-replacement level of infraction, as the point of OOT is to prevent players from discussing the game outside of the confines of the game in terms of mechanics, not like player emotion and the like. We clearly don't want mafia to communicate with each other in the daytime when they don't have daychat, or for players to get outside help in terms of scumhunting and the like. From my experience, I've seen other online games such as Genius ORGs and Survivor ORGs allow this type of stuff as long as it's clear the outside interference does not reveal information to the player that the player would otherwise not have. The point of rules is not to be enforced directly, but to uphold some level of standard; hence why loopholes exist. I feel like this is the opposite of a loophole: breaking the rules, but still upholding the reasoning behind the rules. I only talked to Charge, while Charge did not talk back to me about this game in any way, only just acknowledging that she heard what I said. This is a transfer of information to an outside source, and not the other way around.

If I'm actually force-replaced, I can say it's been a nice time playing with y'all (when I wasn't like constantly being attacked), and I might be back to mod a game with an interesting setup. We'll see. Since I know there's some players here who don't have my Discord, it's e_is_cool#6474 if you want to talk to me or something, since I probably won't be back on AoPS for a bit if I do get replaced.

I'd also like to note that I have yet to get any official statement about the nature of this infraction from benjaminsz.

Me talking to her has honestly given me motivation to continue playing instead of just gamethrowing or just replacing out.

The thing is, I don't get how this is necessarily a force-replacement level of infraction, as the point of OOT is to prevent players from discussing the game outside of the confines of the game in terms of mechanics, not like player emotion and the like. We clearly don't want mafia to communicate with each other in the daytime when they don't have daychat, or for players to get outside help in terms of scumhunting and the like. From my experience, I've seen other online games such as Genius ORGs and Survivor ORGs allow this type of stuff as long as it's clear the outside interference does not reveal information to the player that the player would otherwise not have. The point of rules is not to be enforced directly, but to uphold some level of standard; hence why loopholes exist. I feel like this is the opposite of a loophole: breaking the rules, but still upholding the reasoning behind the rules. I only talked to Charge, while Charge did not talk back to me about this game in any way, only just acknowledging that she heard what I said. This is a transfer of information to an outside source, and not the other way around.

If I'm actually force-replaced, I can say it's been a nice time playing with y'all (when I wasn't like constantly being attacked), and I might be back to mod a game with an interesting setup. We'll see. Since I know there's some players here who don't have my Discord, it's e_is_cool#6474 if you want to talk to me or something, since I probably won't be back on AoPS for a bit if I do get replaced.

I'd also like to note that I have yet to get any official statement about the nature of this infraction from benjaminsz.

post_rendered: Also, I forgot to mention this but I did ask Charge about this before I posted this in regards to revealing her name.
