post_rendered:
Summit has been slipping under the radar, so I did an ISO of his posts. The first thing I noticed is that he really has not been saying anything of real substance. He is basically just @ing people and asking them a question with no follow up afterwards. It seems almost like active lurking, where he has an active presence in the thread, but when one tries to recall what his views were, nothing comes to mind. For most of day 1, he basically expressed no opinions on anyone's alignment, and when his questions get answered, he either brushes it off or says things like "I understand" and then never brings it up again. His chain of posts just seem so disjointed without any real scumhunting in them.

Also, he consistently makes posts in this vien
summitwei wrote:
been busy oops
hopefully will be able to catch up late tonight

But he still barely does anything. While this isn't uncommon for people to do, the sheer frequency of these posts that just say this are abnormally high. I don't know why summit feels the need to constantly remind everyone that he will do things later.

His first real substantive post is this
summitwei wrote:
i'm on mobile, so no formal quotes. if i say that something happens but don't give a location you can ask me. i'm not doing a pbpa because they're wastes of time; please don't call cherry picking or something dumb like that.
also i'm writing this case while doing the reading. only way i can keep track of it

a few of charge's early posts feel pretty awkward to me. don't want to put too much weight into it (since awkward for me can still be normal for someone else) but it's at least worth noting.

the entire claiming towniness debacle is something i originally thought wouldn't be very notable but now i'm pretty sure it is. it gets to the point where charge is basically attacking herself at several points to make her cases. this feels like a twtbtwtbaw moment (basically scum trying to do something town would do because it's too weird for scum to do it). but i really don't have a satisfying explanation for why; i'll continue thinking though

charge's analyses (137 is the first example) tend to have an interesting trend: almost all of the reads mentioned are scum rather than town. when they are townreads (see the reference to 84 in that message) they're incredibly short and unexplained. there's a pretty obvious scum!charge explanation here: attacking people is good, but don't want to purely attack people since you'll get called out for that, so do the bare minimum of townreading. i should go and check to see if this is how charge plays as town but not sure if i'll have time for it
after writing this i did remember that the aforementioned 137 was supposed to be almost satirical. perhaps some better examples would be better but the trend holds
i guess the question from there is how *bad* this really is? and i feel like it should be bad though i'm pretty sure i've seen town do this before. don't think it's a nullifying factor though; i still don't like it

adding to this: there are some townreads gradually (e.g. on panda in 184). but the progression on panda feels sudden, going from a very careful "isn't scummy" to "genuinely towny" quite quickly. writing a note to keep an eye out for other weird progressions

i'm also quite confused by charge's cases since many of the arguments i see are not very good? like there are many things that are, to me, clearly not "fake contribution" that get blanket slapped under that tag. the example i saw just now was the response to fdas in #178. (on a side note, i remember at least a few other people also doing this stuff? maybe panda? and i wanted to ask a broader question about it at some point but i don't think i ever did so. anyways end side note)
this kind of occurrence comes out of an "attacking-for-the-sake-of-attacking" mentality quite often; i shouldn't have to explain why that's bad.

i'm getting to the point where "fancy omgus" is getting mentioned; i don't think that's bad by itself. what is bad is when you do that, get like one townread, and barely even try to look at anybody else

i see a read list in #321! and it's disappointing. i get one more town read that's completely unexplained (which isn't something i'd expect from charge) and a "i'm not scumreading hang candidates" which feels like the definition of keeping options open. (although by that point i can't even tell who "hang candidates" refers to? that could change things depending on if i'm misreading)

aaaaand that seems to be it; the main case against charge is that her reads are very much negative and don't seem to be coming from the motivation to hunt scum. it's definitely not a good look and i can say i'm definitely ok with this hang happening

(i'll probably end up voting apw at the end of the day; due to deadline hang being deadline hang, i'm not optimistic that a charge hang can happen. lots of stuff worth keeping in mind for tomorrow though)


future summit note that charge-fdas is too easy to be right but still might be right; some *weird* interactions between the two. won't read into this yet but should put it somewhere and i'm typing here rn sooooo

(sidenote: i saw #261 and that answers the question i had about the metaprogression on aroodman. thanks summit for the great explanation to your own question)



uh now that i've written a bunch of nonsense time to post it without proofreading cuz it's past midnight and i want sleep so i don't silly every other question on amc tmrw

He rambles on and on about Charge and things he finds scummy, yet at the end all he says is
summitwei wrote:
aaaaand that seems to be it; the main case against charge is that her reads are very much negative and don't seem to be coming from the motivation to hunt scum. it's definitely not a good look and i can say i'm definitely ok with this hang happening

(i'll probably end up voting apw at the end of the day; due to deadline hang being deadline hang, i'm not optimistic that a charge hang can happen. lots of stuff worth keeping in mind for tomorrow though)

He says he is ok with the hang happening but makes no effort whatsoever to push. This is about as fencesitting as you can get. Even after saying all this he doesn't take a hard stance or even put down a vote. summit is literally voting zdtsd at this point. His "attack" on Charge is half hearted at best.

This is the last post that summit makes before apw claims

From here, summit's activity turns around completely and once the roleclaim controversy comes up summit talks so much more. While it is towny that summit at least took a clear and committed stance on the controversy, this is the first time he took a stand all game. And this is a very low risk discussion for him to take part in. It is easy to talk mechanics rather than actually scumhunt and given a much easier conversation topic, summit no longer lurks and is now liberally expressing his opinions.

But then, after the day ends and the game moves back into normal scumhunting, summit immediately no longer has anything to say

And then he goes back to making posts like this
summitwei wrote:
this game is too hard

i don't have the time for an ISO and nobody's posting


Overall, summit has been lurking the entire game and making no effort to scumhunt or contribute. And he has not been called out for it and seems to have succeeded completely at not expressing views and just going with the flow.

Proof that Red is scum:

Assume Red's claim is true.

Since she healed apw night 1, apw must either have shot twice or red was roleblocked.
Mafia would not have roleblocked Red when the 1 shot cop had claimed, since they had no way of knowing red was the doctor.

We arrive at a contradiction, since the setup has no SK.

Therefore Red's claim is false and she is scum.

post_rendered:
OK

here's my extended post on the e replacement situation

I'm not going to go into any more details than have already been revealed on why e received a warning, since discussion with the SE's reveals no one actually contests that and I'd like to make sure there is no backlash against charge, who handled the situation excellently.

Since the actual controversy is about one warning being grounds for replacement, I will instead explain my thought process on that.

Every OP since I can remember has "3 warnings for newbie, 2 for SE, 1 for IC". Now, I never read this super closely, so my initial interpretation was that that meant "you get this many strikes and you're out" - and you can see evidence of me acting on this in past games, such as stating that a newbie with two warnings was in danger of being force-replaced/modkilled.

Since I discovered after the fact that that wasn't actually what my OP said, I changed it - knowing that this was a shady action - because I was under the impression that I was following a universal forum rule. I now know that no such rule exists - and if we are to enact actual rules-enforcement reform, we should create one. To say "3 warnings, the force-replace/MK, unless a game-breaking infraction is committed, in which case there will be immediate force-replace/MK". For everyone, regardless of experience.

post_rendered:
uh so...

I personally think that when I was playing I was absolutely killing it. just gotta say

anyways, it's been a while now so I forgot everything I was thinking.

The gist of it was: nothing went like I thought it would. Obviously I was townread by a lot of people, so there was no reason to just die. When I claimed, I was 100% sure that fdas was SK. Like for real. At first I thought fdas would get killed without me saying anything but then town started to backtrack and consider his vig claim as true. As someone who believed fdas was SK, I could not let that happen--he HAD to die that day. So I claimed as doctor (rolling the dice that the remaining role was not a roleblocker).

I think the main reason I believed fdas couldn't not be the SK was that the real SK had to have double-killed chess again which I saw as highly unlikely. I thought it would be way too ballsy to claim a kill that wasn't yours so I couldn't believe that he wasn't SK. I was 100% shocked when he wasn't SK.

Anyways, so that obviously foiled the logic behind my claim--I thought that the real doctor would cc me, and I would have the benefit of the townread on my side, win the cc, and do in the last SK and doctor with my claim. None of that happened.

So I really had to go with the flow and do my best but obviously the logic of the setup did me in. idk whether aroodman would have been able to think up such a claim as well but I think it would have obviously been more optimal if he had done it instead of me but you know I can't communicate all that during the day.

Hope this makes sense. After I was out the game was pretty reset and I had no control over the rest of the game.

aroodman: ngl your lylo was weak and it was pretty ez clap for jeremy but you're lucky thorn wasn't trying. Even if Hannah was tr'ed at the end I think in your position I would have actually killed jeremy because IMO there was no way he was still scum at that point. Then you could battle Hannah to decide between you or thorn and I think that was a fairer battle.

Something I wrote over two months ago: By the way, I would like to mention something regarding how e's OOT was treated after it had already happened. Something I disapprove of and am disappointed by in both the mods and the players is the dismissal of e's feelings--yes, I also agree that for me personally, it would be tough to feel substantial emotional distress from a mafia game because it is "just a game" and being attacked "comes along with signing up for the game". But you cannot determine what other people feel, and you cannot dismiss other people's feelings offhand because you do not share that experience or that's the way things "should" or "shouldn't" be. So I just wanted to say to e, I'm sorry for what happened to you and how people, including myself, did not take you seriously enough or compassionately understand your feelings.

/fin thanks for letting me come back to this forum for some fun
